item.title: The world-class Zimbabwe farm that Robert Mugabe’s wife received has collapsed and been sold for spare parts
item.description: Foyle Farm, under Ian Webster, was world-class and supplied a significant portion of Zimbabwe’s national dairy needs. However, the Mugabe family destroyed the farm. Foyle Farm was the original name of the property that became the centrepiece of the Mugabe family’s agricultural empire in the Mazowe Valley. Before its takeover, it was widely regarded as […]The post The world-class Zimbabwe farm that Robert Mugabe’s wife received has collapsed and been sold for spare parts appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Rural Industrialisation Is a Costly Illusion: Why Zimbabwe Must Focus on Urban Industrial Growth, Depopulate Rural Areas
item.description: THE push for “rural industrialisation” is gaining rhetorical momentum in Zimbabwe. It should not. However well-intentioned, it runs against the grain of how modern economies actually develop and risks scattering scarce capital across locations that cannot support a competitive industry and a competitive economy. By Brighton Musonza The record is remarkably consistent. Successful industrialisation has […]The post Rural Industrialisation Is a Costly Illusion: Why Zimbabwe Must Focus on Urban Industrial Growth, Depopulate Rural Areas appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Etihad Airways Entry Seen as Major Boost for Zimbabwe’s Connectivity and Trade Ambitions
item.description: Harare — Zimbabwe’s aviation and trade sectors are poised for renewed momentum following the announcement by Etihad Airways of its expansion into the Zimbabwean market, a move widely viewed by industry experts as a strategic breakthrough for the country’s global connectivity and economic positioning. The Abu Dhabi-based carrier has confirmed the launch of new African […]The post Etihad Airways Entry Seen as Major Boost for Zimbabwe’s Connectivity and Trade Ambitions appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe Economy Holds Steady as Global Shocks Test Resilienc
item.description: Harare — Zimbabwe’s economy maintained a steady growth trajectory in the first quarter of 2026, demonstrating resilience in the face of intensifying global geopolitical tensions, even as rising costs linked to the Middle East conflict ripple through international markets. A detailed review by FBC Securities indicates that while external shocks—particularly surging energy and agricultural input […]The post Zimbabwe Economy Holds Steady as Global Shocks Test Resilienc appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Tim Cook will step down as Apple CEO and hand reins over to the iPhone maker’s hardware leader
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook is stepping down from the job that he inherited from the late Steve Jobs, ending a 15-year reign that saw the company’s market value soar by more than $3.6 trillion during an iPhone-fueled era of prosperity. Cook, 65, will turn the CEO duties over to Apple’s head of hardware engineering, John […]The post Tim Cook will step down as Apple CEO and hand reins over to the iPhone maker’s hardware leader appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Oil prices slip and world shares mostly gain as US-Iran talks still in doubt
item.description: Oil prices slipped and shares were mostly higher Tuesday in Europe and Asia as U.S.-Iran talks aimed at ending the war remained in doubt. The price for a barrel of Brent crude oil dipped 0.7% to $94.81. U.S. benchmark crude oil lost 0.9% to $86.63 per barrel. The war has disrupted transport of oil through […]The post Oil prices slip and world shares mostly gain as US-Iran talks still in doubt appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe’s Currency Experiment: Between Theoretical Valuation and Market Reality
item.description: Zimbabwe’s latest monetary reform, the Zimbabwe Gold (ZiG), is not merely another policy shift; it is a continuation of a long and difficult search for monetary sovereignty in an economy that has, over time, defaulted to the United States Dollar as its dominant medium of exchange. The central tension is clear: policymakers are attempting to […]The post Zimbabwe’s Currency Experiment: Between Theoretical Valuation and Market Reality appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe’s Central Bank Says Its Currency Deeply Undervalued
item.description: Despite efforts at stabilization and reduced inflation, over 90% of transactions in Zimbabwe are still conducted in US dollars. Inflation has dropped to single digits for the first time in nearly 30 years, though annual inflation slightly increased to 4.4% in March. Zimbabwe’s central bank governor says the country’s currency is significantly undervalued, arguing that […]The post Zimbabwe’s Central Bank Says Its Currency Deeply Undervalued appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Health minister Mombeshora pleads with striking nurses to return to work
item.description: HARARE — Health minister Douglas Mombeshora on Monday appealed to striking nurses to suspend their job action on the first day of a three-day walkout over pay. Nurses walked out at hospitals across the country, accusing the government of paying lip service to salary demands. They earn an average of US$290 and ZiG5,000 per month, […]The post Health minister Mombeshora pleads with striking nurses to return to work appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.item: Zimbabwe at 46: Reflections on the struggle to deepen electoral democracy https://www.mutapatimes.com/articles/2026-04-17-zimbabwe-at-46-reflections-on-the-struggle-to-deepen-elector.html ‘We must confront every idea which keeps us polarised and outward-looking’ The post Zimbabwe at 46: Reflections on the struggle to deepen electoral democracy appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now . Wed, 22 Apr 2026 13:16:30 +0000 https://www.mutapatimes.com/articles/2026-04-17-zimbabwe-at-46-reflections-on-the-struggle-to-deepen-elector.html Business Zimbabwean News <media:content url="summary: "‘We must confront every idea which keeps us polarised and outward-looking’ The post Zimbabwe at 46: Reflections on the struggle to deepen electoral democracy appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now ." medium="image"/>

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Bloodbath in Warren Park: Harare’s Most Feared Armed Robbers SHOT DEAD in Broad Daylight After Shootout with Police
item.description: From Glenview to the Grave: Betrayal and Bullets—How a Police Informant Led to the Downfall of Harare’s Most Ruthless Armed Robbery Syndicate! HARARE – The afternoon sun was still high over the Warren Park 1 Shopping Centre on a Wednesday that began like any other in the bustling
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News
